description: P1-12: the PUBLISHED RiNALMo ArchiveII nine-family inter-family (leave-one-family-out) secondary-structure F1 scores — the parity target for the P1-13 mirror parity gate (PRD §10.2; ADR-0002 D5). Values are the NON-WEIGHTED F1 column (per-structure simple mean) from Supplementary Table S2; the length-weighted column differs (SRP 0.628, groupI 0.675, 16S 0.721, mean 0.712) and is NOT the parity target. Sourced from the paper, not fabricated (CLAUDE.md §10.3).

source.paper: Penic et al., RiNALMo: general-purpose RNA language models can generalize well on structure prediction tasks, Nature Communications 2025
source.location: Supplementary Table S2, RiNALMo F1_non_weighted column ('Inter-family generalization evaluation ... F1 scores for secondary structure prediction')
corroboration: Supp. Table S2 (F1 non-weighted) — primary numeric source
Supp. Table S3 (INF, an independent metric) — same per-family pattern incl. telomerase collapse
Main-text Results + Fig. 4a — qualitative (RiNALMo wins 8/9 families, 'struggles to generalize on telomerase RNAs')
Independent benchmark arXiv:2410.16212 (preprint, not peer-reviewed) — corroboration only; same pattern (tRNA highest, telomerase lowest)
source.verification: scientific-evidence gate (CLAUDE.md §10.1) CLEARED: >=3 corroborating reads incl. an independent pymupdf extraction of the SI PDF; every value reproduced exactly and matches ADR-0002 D5 to 2 dp.

metric.name: base-pair F1
metric.aggregation: per-structure F1 averaged un-weighted within each held-out family (non-weighted column)

sd_note: The paper reports NO numeric per-split/per-family F1 standard deviation (Fig. 4b shows box/violin distributions only; the Table S2 'Mean/STD' columns are sequence-LENGTH statistics, not F1 SD). Therefore the PRD §10.2 primary criterion ('within published SD') is not usable and the ADR-0002 D5 +/-N pp fallback governs.

telomerase_carveout.reason: near the failure floor + high variance (acknowledged outlier); excluded from the strict gate or given a widened +/-5-10 pp band (ADR-0002 D5).

parity_gate.note: N=2 pp covers ~1 pp printed-resolution rounding + ~1 pp same-weights fine-tuning/reimplementation noise, without admitting a meaningful accuracy gap. A >2 pp shift on the mean or on multiple stable families is a real reimplementation discrepancy to investigate, not accepted.

adr_pinned_f1_2dp.agreement: published F1 (Table S2 non-weighted) matches ADR-0002 D5 to 2 dp for all ten values.

dataset.counts_note: per-family n_test counts derived by extraction 2026-07-13 (not quoted from the literature, which disagrees on the small families).
